items.title: Kelun-Merck validate TROP2-Keytruda pairing in lung cancer with improved survival
items.snippet: Treatment with the TROP2 ADC sac-TMT led to a 70% objective response rate and progression-free survival was “significantly improved” as compared to placebo—the second positive readout for the asset this week.

items.title: Recordati fields $12.4B go-private offer
items.snippet: Going private could give Recordati strategic flexibility and a stable source of capital, according to CVC Capital Partners and Groupe Bruxelles Lambert, which are offering to take the Italian pharma private for a 13% premium.

items.title: Gilead inks $140M API supply deal, tightening ties to Korean manufacturer
items.snippet: The agreement is the largest in a series of deals that Gilead Sciences has penned with Korea’s Yuhan.

items.title: AbbVie, Novartis slash US headcount
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Biogen, Denali pull plug on Parkinson’s program after mid-stage flop
items.snippet: Biogen and Denali’s Parkinson’s disease drug failed to significantly slow disease progression in a Phase 2b study, missing both primary and secondary endpoints.

items.title: Moderna’s once-rebuffed mRNA flu shot to face scrutiny from FDA adcomm
items.snippet: After getting slapped with a surprise refuse-to-file letter signed by former CBER Director Vinay Prasad, Moderna’s flu vaccine application will now go before the FDA’s Vaccine advisory committee.

items.title: How To Shine As Biotech Comes Back From the Brink
items.snippet: Biotech has been pulling off some remarkable science of late, while M&A and IPOs return to bolster the sector. But that doesn’t mean the industry is back from the brink. BioSpace gathered three early-stage biotech CEOs and members of the NextGen Class of 2026 to talk about surviving and thriving in this rollercoaster...

items.title: Update: Reversal after 20 deaths allows new Japan patients to take Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos
items.snippet: Kissei Pharmaceutical is reversing a recommendation related to Amgen-shared Tavneos that it made just a few days ago, now saying the rare disease drug can be given to new patients.

items.title: Roche licensing pact clears generic drugmakers to manufacture antiviral flu drug
items.snippet: By partnering with a UN-backed body, Roche has enabled companies to make the medicine for supply in 129 countries.

items.title: Lilly’s triple agonist shows bariatric surgery–like weight loss results
items.snippet: Lilly met analysts’ sky-high expectations with 28.3% weight loss over 80 weeks for the triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide in a highly anticipated readout on Thursday.

items.title: Vaxart seeks shareholder support as battle over stock split brews
items.snippet: In September last year, a group of concerned stockholders raised alarm about Vaxart’s proposed reverse stock split, which the biotech was pushing for despite strong opposition from shareholders.

items.title: Lilly, BMS, Incyte strike deals to keep biopharma’s AI integration rolling
items.snippet: A trifecta of newly inked tech partnerships—from Eli Lilly, Bristol Myers Squibb and Incyte—exemplify the increasingly central role that AI is playing in drug development.

items.title: BioMarin bounces back as Voxzogo performs ‘bigger than expected’ in new indication
items.snippet: After being hit by safety issues and subpar results in another trial, BioMarin’s Phase 3 test of Voxzogo for a rare skeletal disorder called hypochondroplasia showed efficacy “solidly above” what the drug has shown for achondroplasia, which causes dwarfism.

items.title: Inside Germany’s biotech capital infrastructure and innovation engine
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, as part of our series on the European life sciences investment ecosystem, you'll be hearing from Regina Hodits, managing director at Angelini Ventures and Sofia Ioannidou, VC partner at Andera Life Sciences. They explore Germany’s biotech and life sciences ecosystem, including the science...

items.title: Biopharma professionals increasingly accept underemployment
items.snippet: The number of biotech and pharma professionals taking jobs they’re overqualified for is now over 50%, based on a BioSpace LinkedIn poll. A recruiting firm executive discusses the trend, the reasons behind it and why it doesn’t have to derail careers.

items.title: Lilly and Engage Bio tie the knot with $202M acquisition
items.snippet: Eli Lilly joins hands with Engage Bio, acquiring the DNA delivery platform developer in hopes of bolstering its genetic medicines portfolio.

items.title: FDA sends warning letter after Chinese supplier breaks GLP-1 import restrictions
items.snippet: While the manufacturer is on the list of authorized GLP-1 importers, FDA inspectors found the company relabeled APIs from another site in a potential attempt to “circumvent safeguards.”

items.title: Pazdur declines to announce candidacy for vacated FDA commissioner role
items.snippet: FDA veteran Richard Pazdur outlined his priorities for the next FDA commissioner but stopped short of putting himself forward as a candidate to lead the agency at an RBC Capital Markets event on Tuesday.

items.title: Moderna’s stock rides hantavirus fears as spotlight returns to infectious disease platform
items.snippet: While the pathogen appears unlikely to trigger a pandemic, analysts see potential for Moderna to build goodwill amid a period of political pressure on vaccine manufacturers.

items.title: Beam one-ups Wave as both show promise of genetic editing for AATD
items.snippet: While both Beam Therapeutics and Wave Life Sciences touted notable biomarker benefits for their respective alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency assets, analysts said that Beam might have the efficacy advantage as Wave’s drug hits an efficacy “ceiling.”

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.
